Container.Name: gluetun-companion
Container.Overview:
Gluetun Companion is a web UI that controls an existing Gluetun container: server catalogue, benchmarks, automatic/manual switches, Docker dependent recreation, VPN port forwarding rules, qBittorrent/rTorrent synchronization and Prometheus metrics.
Unraid notes: this template is made for DockerMan-managed Gluetun containers. Companion needs read/write access to /boot/config/plugins/dockerMan/templates-user so server switches persist in the Unraid template XML. It also needs the Docker socket to inspect/recreate Gluetun and its running network dependents. Because DockerMan templates are root-owned and mode 600 on Unraid, the container intentionally runs as root; PUID/PGID are not exposed because they would break template write-back unless the host permissions are changed manually.
Before first start, set SECRET_KEY to a long random value and keep it forever. Changing it later makes encrypted VPN credentials unreadable. Example: openssl rand -hex 32
Container.Requires: Existing Gluetun container managed by Unraid DockerMan. Gluetun HTTP proxy is recommended for proxy tests; Gluetun Control Server is required for native port forwarding synchronization.

Config.Name: WebUI
Config.Description: Gluetun Companion web interface. The WebUI entry intentionally references the container port so Unraid follows any host port remap.

Config.Name: Appdata
Config.Description: Persistent Companion database, encrypted VPN profiles, benchmark history and settings.

Config.Name: Docker Socket
Config.Description: Required to inspect Gluetun, create sidecar test containers, watch Docker events and recreate running containers attached to Gluetun network namespace.

Config.Name: Unraid DockerMan templates
Config.Description: Required in Unraid mode. Companion updates the Gluetun DockerMan XML template so VPN switches persist across Unraid recreates/updates.

Config.Name: Gluetun appdata
Config.Description: Read-only access to Gluetun local server catalogues, especially /gluetun/servers.json and /gluetun/servers/*.json. This lets Companion import the exact server list used by the installed Gluetun container instead of falling back to the public GitHub catalogue.

Config.Name: SECRET_KEY
Config.Description: Required. Generate once with: openssl rand -hex 32. Keep it stable; changing it makes encrypted VPN credentials unreadable.

Config.Name: Timezone
Config.Description: Timezone used for logs and UI timestamps.

Config.Name: Gluetun container name
Config.Description: Exact name of the existing Gluetun container managed by Unraid DockerMan.

Config.Name: Gluetun proxy host
Config.Description: Host used by Companion to reach Gluetun HTTP proxy from inside this container. For the standard Unraid bridge setup, keep host.docker.internal.

Config.Name: Gluetun HTTP proxy host port
Config.Description: Host port published by Gluetun for its HTTP proxy. Used for proxy quick checks and public IP detection. Match the host port mapped to Gluetun container port 8888.

Config.Name: Sidecar image
Config.Description: Sidecar image used for speed tests and catalogue imports. Keep it aligned with the Companion image tag.

Config.Name: Custom OpenVPN config folder
Config.Description: Optional. Folder where Companion can store uploaded Custom OpenVPN profiles. It should be the same host folder mounted into Gluetun.

Config.Name: DATA_DIR
Config.Description: Internal data directory. Do not change unless you also change the Appdata mount target.

Config.Name: COMPOSE_DIR
Config.Description: Internal compose directory for non-Unraid compose mode. DockerMan-managed Unraid Gluetun containers do not need this mount.

Config.Name: OPENVPN_CONFIG_DIR
Config.Description: Internal path where Companion writes uploaded Custom OpenVPN files.

Config.Name: OPENVPN_CONTAINER_DIR
Config.Description: Path where the same OpenVPN folder is visible from inside the Gluetun container.

Config.Name: UNRAID_TEMPLATE_DIR
Config.Description: Internal path to Unraid DockerMan user templates. Keep aligned with the DockerMan templates mount target.

Config.Name: METRICS_TOKEN
Config.Description: Optional Bearer token for /metrics. Leave empty for LAN-only Prometheus scraping or set a strong token if exposed through a reverse proxy.

Config.Name: LOG_LEVEL
Config.Description: Logging verbosity: INFO for normal use, DEBUG while troubleshooting.

Config.Name: LOG_JSON
Config.Description: Set to 1 to emit structured JSON logs.
